1	# coding=utf-8
2	# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	"""RolloutWorker: PromptTask -> features/refs -> SampleRef commit.
10	
11	The worker is deliberately small and algorithm-agnostic: it leases prompt tasks,
12	asks an external-server ``feature_source`` for per-sample features,
13	verifies them against the typed ``CaptureConfig`` *before* writing, writes them
14	to the ``FeatureStore``, and commits the resulting ``SampleRef`` metadata to the
15	controller. A server-side capture source may instead return already-written
16	``SampleRef``s via ``produce_refs``. It never hands a tensor to the controller.
17	Strategy-specific capture requirements live in ``CaptureConfig`` + the feature
18	schema, not here.
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol
24	
25	from specforge.inference.batch_partition import TargetBatchPartition
26	from specforge.inference.capture import (
27	    CaptureConfig,
28	    CaptureMismatchError,
29	    verify_capture,
30	)
31	from specforge.runtime.contracts import PromptTask, SampleRef
32	
33	# health states: a worker REPORTS health; the controller decides scheduling.
34	HEALTH_STATES = ("starting", "ready", "paused", "draining", "unhealthy", "stopped")
35	
36	
37	class FeatureSource(Protocol):
38	    def generate_features(
39	        self, tasks: List[PromptTask], *, capture: CaptureConfig
40	    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: ...
41	
42	
43	class RefSource(Protocol):
44	    def produce_refs(
45	        self, tasks: List[PromptTask], *, capture: CaptureConfig
46	    ) -> List[Any]: ...
47	
48	
49	class RolloutWorker:
50	    def __init__(
51	        self,
52	        controller,
53	        feature_store,
54	        feature_source: FeatureSource,
55	        capture: CaptureConfig,
56	        *,
57	        run_id: str,
58	        worker_id: Optional[str] = None,
59	        strategy: str = "eagle3",
60	        target_model_version: str = "unknown",
61	        tokenizer_version: str = "unknown",
62	        draft_weight_version: Optional[str] = None,
63	        batch_partition: Optional[TargetBatchPartition] = None,
64	        feature_source_returns_local_batch: bool = False,
65	    ) -> None:
66	        self.controller = controller
67	        self.feature_store = feature_store
68	        self.feature_source = feature_source
69	        self.capture = capture
70	        self.run_id = run_id
71	        self.strategy = strategy
72	        self.target_model_version = target_model_version
73	        self.tokenizer_version = tokenizer_version
74	        self.draft_weight_version = draft_weight_version
75	        self.batch_partition = batch_partition or TargetBatchPartition()
76	        self.feature_source_returns_local_batch = bool(
77	            feature_source_returns_local_batch
78	        )
79	        self._state = "starting"
80	        self._inflight = 0
81	        self._recent_failures: List[str] = []
82	        self._last_commit_count = 0
83	        self.worker_id = controller.register_rollout_worker(
84	            {"worker_id": worker_id, "strategy": strategy, "role": "rollout"}
85	        )
86	
87	    def start(self) -> None:
88	        self._state = "ready"
89	
90	    def stop(self, reason: str = "stopped") -> None:
91	        # graceful: finish in-flight, then mark stopped (drain happens in run_once)
92	        self._state = "stopped"
93	
94	    def _sample_id(self, task: PromptTask) -> str:
95	        return f"{self.run_id}:{task.task_id}"
96	
97	    def run_once(self, max_tasks: int) -> List[SampleRef]:
98	        if self._state in ("stopped", "draining"):
99	            return []
100	        tasks = self.controller.lease_prompt_tasks(self.worker_id, max_tasks)
101	        if not tasks:
102	            return []
103	        self._inflight = len(tasks)
104	        self._state = "ready"
105	        # Legacy colocated target-TP training used a drop-last target batch.
106	        # A partial target batch would give ranks different local batch sizes
107	        # and break the all-rank draft/FSDP step contract.
108	        if self.batch_partition.size > 1 and len(tasks) != max_tasks:
109	            self.controller.complete_prompt_tasks(
110	                self.worker_id, [task.task_id for task in tasks]
111	            )
112	            self._inflight = 0
113	            return []
114	
115	        local_slice = self.batch_partition.local_slice(len(tasks))
116	        local_tasks = list(tasks[local_slice])
117	        peer_tasks = list(tasks[: local_slice.start]) + list(tasks[local_slice.stop :])
118	        produce_refs = getattr(self.feature_source, "produce_refs", None)
119	        if callable(produce_refs):
120	            if self.batch_partition.size > 1:
121	                raise NotImplementedError(
122	                    "target-TP batch partitioning is supported for colocated "
123	                    "feature capture only"
124	                )
125	            return self._run_ref_source(tasks, produce_refs)
126	
127	        try:
128	            feats_list = self.feature_source.generate_features(
129	                tasks, capture=self.capture
130	            )
131	        except Exception as exc:  # rollout failure before any feature write
132	            self._state = "unhealthy"
133	            self._recent_failures.append(f"generate_features: {exc}")
134	            self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
135	                self.worker_id,
136	                [t.task_id for t in tasks],
137	                reason=f"generate_features:{exc}",
138	                retryable=True,
139	            )
140	            self._inflight = 0
141	            raise
142	        expected_count = (
143	            len(local_tasks) if self.feature_source_returns_local_batch else len(tasks)
144	        )
145	        if len(feats_list) != expected_count:
146	            reason = (
147	                f"generate_features returned {len(feats_list)} feature records "
148	                f"for {expected_count} expected records"
149	            )
150	            self._state = "unhealthy"
151	            self._recent_failures.append(reason)
152	            self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
153	                self.worker_id,
154	                [t.task_id for t in tasks],
155	                reason=reason,
156	                retryable=False,
157	            )
158	            self._inflight = 0
159	            raise ValueError(reason)
160	
161	        local_feats = (
162	            feats_list
163	            if self.feature_source_returns_local_batch
164	            else list(feats_list[local_slice])
165	        )
166	        if peer_tasks:
167	            self.controller.complete_prompt_tasks(
168	                self.worker_id, [task.task_id for task in peer_tasks]
169	            )
170	
171	        refs: List[SampleRef] = []
172	        capture_error: Optional[CaptureMismatchError] = None
173	        put_error: Optional[Exception] = None
174	        for task, feats in zip(local_tasks, local_feats):
175	            sample_id = self._sample_id(task)
176	            recorded = feats.pop("__aux_layer_ids__", None)
177	            try:
178	                verify_capture(
179	                    feats,
180	                    self.capture,
181	                    sample_id=sample_id,
182	                    recorded_aux_layer_ids=recorded,
183	                )
184	            except CaptureMismatchError as exc:
185	                # Loud failure: do not persist a corrupt sample, but keep this
186	                # batch's other prompt leases moving so no lease is stranded.
187	                self._recent_failures.append(str(exc))
188	                self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
189	                    self.worker_id, [task.task_id], reason=str(exc), retryable=False
190	                )
191	                if capture_error is None:
192	                    capture_error = exc
193	                continue
194	            try:
195	                ref = self.feature_store.put(
196	                    feats,
197	                    sample_id=sample_id,
198	                    metadata=self._put_metadata(task),
199	                )
200	            except Exception as exc:  # partial write -> abort, report
201	                self.feature_store.abort(sample_id, reason=f"put_failed:{exc}")
202	                self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
203	                    self.worker_id,
204	                    [task.task_id],
205	                    reason=str(exc),
206	                    # A target-TP peer cannot recapture this one prompt alone
207	                    # without desynchronizing the next collective target batch.
208	                    retryable=self.batch_partition.size == 1,
209	                )
210	                if self.batch_partition.size > 1 and put_error is None:
211	                    put_error = exc
212	                continue
213	            refs.append(ref)
214	
215	        if refs:
216	            self.controller.commit_samples(self.worker_id, refs)
217	            self._last_commit_count += len(refs)
218	        self._inflight = 0
219	        if capture_error is not None:
220	            self._state = "unhealthy"
221	            raise capture_error
222	        if put_error is not None:
223	            self._state = "unhealthy"
224	            raise RuntimeError("target-TP local feature write failed") from put_error
225	        return refs
226	
227	    def _run_ref_source(self, tasks: List[PromptTask], produce_refs) -> List[SampleRef]:
228	        try:
229	            results = produce_refs(tasks, capture=self.capture)
230	        except Exception as exc:
231	            self._state = "unhealthy"
232	            self._recent_failures.append(f"produce_refs: {exc}")
233	            self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
234	                self.worker_id,
235	                [t.task_id for t in tasks],
236	                reason=f"produce_refs:{exc}",
237	                retryable=True,
238	            )
239	            self._inflight = 0
240	            raise
241	        if len(results) != len(tasks):
242	            reason = (
243	                f"produce_refs returned {len(results)} records for {len(tasks)} tasks"
244	            )
245	            self._state = "unhealthy"
246	            self._recent_failures.append(reason)
247	            self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
248	                self.worker_id,
249	                [t.task_id for t in tasks],
250	                reason=reason,
251	                retryable=False,
252	            )
253	            self._inflight = 0
254	            raise ValueError(reason)
255	
256	        refs: List[SampleRef] = []
257	        for result in results:
258	            if isinstance(result, SampleRef):
259	                refs.append(result)
260	                continue
261	
262	            task_id = getattr(result, "task_id", None)
263	            reason = getattr(
264	                result,
265	                "reason",
266	                f"produce_refs returned {type(result).__name__}, not SampleRef",
267	            )
268	            retryable = bool(getattr(result, "retryable", True))
269	            if task_id is None:
270	                self._state = "unhealthy"
271	                self._recent_failures.append(reason)
272	                self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
273	                    self.worker_id,
274	                    [t.task_id for t in tasks],
275	                    reason=reason,
276	                    retryable=False,
277	                )
278	                self._inflight = 0
279	                raise TypeError(reason)
280	            self._recent_failures.append(str(reason))
281	            self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
282	                self.worker_id, [task_id], reason=str(reason), retryable=retryable
283	            )
284	
285	        if refs:
286	            self.controller.commit_samples(self.worker_id, refs)
287	            self._last_commit_count += len(refs)
288	        self._inflight = 0
289	        return refs
290	
291	    def _put_metadata(self, task: PromptTask) -> Dict[str, Any]:
292	        return {
293	            "run_id": self.run_id,
294	            "source_task_id": task.task_id,
295	            "strategy": self.strategy,
296	            "target_repr": self.capture.target_repr,
297	            "vocab_map_version": self.capture.vocab_map_version,
298	            "ttt_length": self.capture.extra.get("ttt_length"),
299	            "target_model_version": self.target_model_version,
300	            "tokenizer_version": self.tokenizer_version,
301	            "draft_weight_version": self.draft_weight_version,
302	            "num_tokens": int(task.metadata.get("num_tokens", 0)),
303	        }
304	
305	    def drain(self) -> None:
306	        """Stop leasing new work; in-flight is finished by the active run_once."""
307	        self._state = "draining"
308	
309	    # Draft-weight hot update (update_weights -> adapter) is not yet supported.
310	    # draft_weight_version is still recorded as rollout provenance on each sample.
311	
312	    def health(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
313	        return {
314	            "worker_id": self.worker_id,
315	            "state": self._state,
316	            "strategy": self.strategy,
317	            "draft_weight_version": self.draft_weight_version,
318	            "in_flight": self._inflight,
319	            "recent_failures": self._recent_failures[-5:],
320	            "committed": self._last_commit_count,
321	        }
322	
323	
324	__all__ = ["RolloutWorker", "FeatureSource", "RefSource", "HEALTH_STATES"]
325

# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
"""RolloutWorker: PromptTask -> features/refs -> SampleRef commit.

The worker is deliberately small and algorithm-agnostic: it leases prompt tasks,
asks an external-server ``feature_source`` for per-sample features,
verifies them against the typed ``CaptureConfig`` *before* writing, writes them
to the ``FeatureStore``, and commits the resulting ``SampleRef`` metadata to the
controller. A server-side capture source may instead return already-written
``SampleRef``s via ``produce_refs``. It never hands a tensor to the controller.
Strategy-specific capture requirements live in ``CaptureConfig`` + the feature
schema, not here.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol

from specforge.inference.batch_partition import TargetBatchPartition
from specforge.inference.capture import (
    CaptureConfig,
    CaptureMismatchError,
    verify_capture,
)
from specforge.runtime.contracts import PromptTask, SampleRef

# health states: a worker REPORTS health; the controller decides scheduling.
HEALTH_STATES = ("starting", "ready", "paused", "draining", "unhealthy", "stopped")


class FeatureSource(Protocol):
    def generate_features(
        self, tasks: List[PromptTask], *, capture: CaptureConfig
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: ...


class RefSource(Protocol):
    def produce_refs(
        self, tasks: List[PromptTask], *, capture: CaptureConfig
    ) -> List[Any]: ...


class RolloutWorker:
    def __init__(
        self,
        controller,
        feature_store,
        feature_source: FeatureSource,
        capture: CaptureConfig,
        *,
        run_id: str,
        worker_id: Optional[str] = None,
        strategy: str = "eagle3",
        target_model_version: str = "unknown",
        tokenizer_version: str = "unknown",
        draft_weight_version: Optional[str] = None,
        batch_partition: Optional[TargetBatchPartition] = None,
        feature_source_returns_local_batch: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        self.controller = controller
        self.feature_store = feature_store
        self.feature_source = feature_source
        self.capture = capture
        self.run_id = run_id
        self.strategy = strategy
        self.target_model_version = target_model_version
        self.tokenizer_version = tokenizer_version
        self.draft_weight_version = draft_weight_version
        self.batch_partition = batch_partition or TargetBatchPartition()
        self.feature_source_returns_local_batch = bool(
            feature_source_returns_local_batch
        )
        self._state = "starting"
        self._inflight = 0
        self._recent_failures: List[str] = []
        self._last_commit_count = 0
        self.worker_id = controller.register_rollout_worker(
            {"worker_id": worker_id, "strategy": strategy, "role": "rollout"}
        )

    def start(self) -> None:
        self._state = "ready"

    def stop(self, reason: str = "stopped") -> None:
        # graceful: finish in-flight, then mark stopped (drain happens in run_once)
        self._state = "stopped"

    def _sample_id(self, task: PromptTask) -> str:
        return f"{self.run_id}:{task.task_id}"

    def run_once(self, max_tasks: int) -> List[SampleRef]:
        if self._state in ("stopped", "draining"):
            return []
        tasks = self.controller.lease_prompt_tasks(self.worker_id, max_tasks)
        if not tasks:
            return []
        self._inflight = len(tasks)
        self._state = "ready"
        # Legacy colocated target-TP training used a drop-last target batch.
        # A partial target batch would give ranks different local batch sizes
        # and break the all-rank draft/FSDP step contract.
        if self.batch_partition.size > 1 and len(tasks) != max_tasks:
            self.controller.complete_prompt_tasks(
                self.worker_id, [task.task_id for task in tasks]
            )
            self._inflight = 0
            return []

        local_slice = self.batch_partition.local_slice(len(tasks))
        local_tasks = list(tasks[local_slice])
        peer_tasks = list(tasks[: local_slice.start]) + list(tasks[local_slice.stop :])
        produce_refs = getattr(self.feature_source, "produce_refs", None)
        if callable(produce_refs):
            if self.batch_partition.size > 1:
                raise NotImplementedError(
                    "target-TP batch partitioning is supported for colocated "
                    "feature capture only"
                )
            return self._run_ref_source(tasks, produce_refs)

        try:
            feats_list = self.feature_source.generate_features(
                tasks, capture=self.capture
            )
        except Exception as exc:  # rollout failure before any feature write
            self._state = "unhealthy"
            self._recent_failures.append(f"generate_features: {exc}")
            self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
                self.worker_id,
                [t.task_id for t in tasks],
                reason=f"generate_features:{exc}",
                retryable=True,
            )
            self._inflight = 0
            raise
        expected_count = (
            len(local_tasks) if self.feature_source_returns_local_batch else len(tasks)
        )
        if len(feats_list) != expected_count:
            reason = (
                f"generate_features returned {len(feats_list)} feature records "
                f"for {expected_count} expected records"
            )
            self._state = "unhealthy"
            self._recent_failures.append(reason)
            self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
                self.worker_id,
                [t.task_id for t in tasks],
                reason=reason,
                retryable=False,
            )
            self._inflight = 0
            raise ValueError(reason)

        local_feats = (
            feats_list
            if self.feature_source_returns_local_batch
            else list(feats_list[local_slice])
        )
        if peer_tasks:
            self.controller.complete_prompt_tasks(
                self.worker_id, [task.task_id for task in peer_tasks]
            )

        refs: List[SampleRef] = []
        capture_error: Optional[CaptureMismatchError] = None
        put_error: Optional[Exception] = None
        for task, feats in zip(local_tasks, local_feats):
            sample_id = self._sample_id(task)
            recorded = feats.pop("__aux_layer_ids__", None)
            try:
                verify_capture(
                    feats,
                    self.capture,
                    sample_id=sample_id,
                    recorded_aux_layer_ids=recorded,
                )
            except CaptureMismatchError as exc:
                # Loud failure: do not persist a corrupt sample, but keep this
                # batch's other prompt leases moving so no lease is stranded.
                self._recent_failures.append(str(exc))
                self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
                    self.worker_id, [task.task_id], reason=str(exc), retryable=False
                )
                if capture_error is None:
                    capture_error = exc
                continue
            try:
                ref = self.feature_store.put(
                    feats,
                    sample_id=sample_id,
                    metadata=self._put_metadata(task),
                )
            except Exception as exc:  # partial write -> abort, report
                self.feature_store.abort(sample_id, reason=f"put_failed:{exc}")
                self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
                    self.worker_id,
                    [task.task_id],
                    reason=str(exc),
                    # A target-TP peer cannot recapture this one prompt alone
                    # without desynchronizing the next collective target batch.
                    retryable=self.batch_partition.size == 1,
                )
                if self.batch_partition.size > 1 and put_error is None:
                    put_error = exc
                continue
            refs.append(ref)

        if refs:
            self.controller.commit_samples(self.worker_id, refs)
            self._last_commit_count += len(refs)
        self._inflight = 0
        if capture_error is not None:
            self._state = "unhealthy"
            raise capture_error
        if put_error is not None:
            self._state = "unhealthy"
            raise RuntimeError("target-TP local feature write failed") from put_error
        return refs

    def _run_ref_source(self, tasks: List[PromptTask], produce_refs) -> List[SampleRef]:
        try:
            results = produce_refs(tasks, capture=self.capture)
        except Exception as exc:
            self._state = "unhealthy"
            self._recent_failures.append(f"produce_refs: {exc}")
            self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
                self.worker_id,
                [t.task_id for t in tasks],
                reason=f"produce_refs:{exc}",
                retryable=True,
            )
            self._inflight = 0
            raise
        if len(results) != len(tasks):
            reason = (
                f"produce_refs returned {len(results)} records for {len(tasks)} tasks"
            )
            self._state = "unhealthy"
            self._recent_failures.append(reason)
            self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
                self.worker_id,
                [t.task_id for t in tasks],
                reason=reason,
                retryable=False,
            )
            self._inflight = 0
            raise ValueError(reason)

        refs: List[SampleRef] = []
        for result in results:
            if isinstance(result, SampleRef):
                refs.append(result)
                continue

            task_id = getattr(result, "task_id", None)
            reason = getattr(
                result,
                "reason",
                f"produce_refs returned {type(result).__name__}, not SampleRef",
            )
            retryable = bool(getattr(result, "retryable", True))
            if task_id is None:
                self._state = "unhealthy"
                self._recent_failures.append(reason)
                self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
                    self.worker_id,
                    [t.task_id for t in tasks],
                    reason=reason,
                    retryable=False,
                )
                self._inflight = 0
                raise TypeError(reason)
            self._recent_failures.append(str(reason))
            self.controller.fail_prompt_tasks(
                self.worker_id, [task_id], reason=str(reason), retryable=retryable
            )

        if refs:
            self.controller.commit_samples(self.worker_id, refs)
            self._last_commit_count += len(refs)
        self._inflight = 0
        return refs

    def _put_metadata(self, task: PromptTask) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "run_id": self.run_id,
            "source_task_id": task.task_id,
            "strategy": self.strategy,
            "target_repr": self.capture.target_repr,
            "vocab_map_version": self.capture.vocab_map_version,
            "ttt_length": self.capture.extra.get("ttt_length"),
            "target_model_version": self.target_model_version,
            "tokenizer_version": self.tokenizer_version,
            "draft_weight_version": self.draft_weight_version,
            "num_tokens": int(task.metadata.get("num_tokens", 0)),
        }

    def drain(self) -> None:
        """Stop leasing new work; in-flight is finished by the active run_once."""
        self._state = "draining"

    # Draft-weight hot update (update_weights -> adapter) is not yet supported.
    # draft_weight_version is still recorded as rollout provenance on each sample.

    def health(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "worker_id": self.worker_id,
            "state": self._state,
            "strategy": self.strategy,
            "draft_weight_version": self.draft_weight_version,
            "in_flight": self._inflight,
            "recent_failures": self._recent_failures[-5:],
            "committed": self._last_commit_count,
        }


__all__ = ["RolloutWorker", "FeatureSource", "RefSource", "HEALTH_STATES"]

# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
"""CaptureConfig: the typed contract for what a rollout must extract (B7/B8).

``capture`` is NOT an untyped ``dict[str, Any]``. It is a frozen config derived
from the resolved algorithm feature contract and draft capture layers. Before
any ``FeatureStore.put`` the rollout runs :func:`verify_capture`, which fails
loudly on a name / aux-layer-id / width / target-dim mismatch — turning what
would otherwise be a confusing downstream trainer bug into an immediate,
localized error at the extraction boundary.

Import-light (stdlib only) so the assertions are unit-testable without a GPU.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Dict, FrozenSet, Mapping, Optional, Tuple

from specforge.runtime.contracts import FeatureSpec, TargetRepr


class CaptureMismatchError(AssertionError):
    """Raised when extracted features do not match the requested CaptureConfig."""


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CaptureConfig:
    feature_names: FrozenSet[str]
    aux_hidden_state_layer_ids: Tuple[int, ...]
    target_repr: Optional[TargetRepr]
    target_hidden_size: int
    target_vocab_size: Optional[int] = None
    draft_vocab_size: Optional[int] = None
    vocab_map_version: Optional[str] = None
    extra: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    @classmethod
    def from_strategy(
        cls,
        required_features,
        aux_hidden_state_layer_ids,
        *,
        target_repr: Optional[TargetRepr],
        target_hidden_size: int,
        target_vocab_size: Optional[int] = None,
        draft_vocab_size: Optional[int] = None,
        vocab_map_version: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> "CaptureConfig":
        return cls(
            feature_names=frozenset(required_features),
            aux_hidden_state_layer_ids=tuple(aux_hidden_state_layer_ids),
            target_repr=target_repr,
            target_hidden_size=int(target_hidden_size),
            target_vocab_size=target_vocab_size,
            draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab_size,
            vocab_map_version=vocab_map_version,
        )

    @property
    def expected_aux_width(self) -> int:
        return len(self.aux_hidden_state_layer_ids) * self.target_hidden_size

    def expected_target_dim(self) -> Optional[int]:
        if self.target_repr == "pruned_logits":
            return self.draft_vocab_size
        if self.target_repr == "logits":
            return self.target_vocab_size
        if self.target_repr == "hidden_state":
            return self.target_hidden_size
        return None


def _verify_capture_shapes(
    shapes: Mapping[str, Tuple[int, ...]],
    capture: CaptureConfig,
    *,
    sample_id: str,
    recorded_aux_layer_ids: Optional[Tuple[int, ...]] = None,
    aux_feature_name: str = "hidden_state",
    target_feature_name: str = "target",
) -> None:
    # (1) all requested feature names present
    missing = sorted(n for n in capture.feature_names if n not in shapes)
    if missing:
        raise CaptureMismatchError(
            f"[{sample_id}] capture missing features {missing}; "
            f"got {sorted(shapes)}; requested {sorted(capture.feature_names)}"
        )

    # (2) recorded aux-layer IDs == requested
    if recorded_aux_layer_ids is not None:
        if tuple(recorded_aux_layer_ids) != capture.aux_hidden_state_layer_ids:
            raise CaptureMismatchError(
                f"[{sample_id}] aux-layer id mismatch: recorded "
                f"{tuple(recorded_aux_layer_ids)} != requested "
                f"{capture.aux_hidden_state_layer_ids}"
            )

    # (3) aux width == len(aux_layer_ids) * target_hidden_size
    if aux_feature_name in shapes and capture.aux_hidden_state_layer_ids:
        shape = tuple(shapes[aux_feature_name])
        if not shape:
            raise CaptureMismatchError(
                f"[{sample_id}] aux feature {aux_feature_name!r} has no dimensions"
            )
        width = int(shape[-1])
        if width != capture.expected_aux_width:
            raise CaptureMismatchError(
                f"[{sample_id}] aux width {width} != "
                f"len(aux_layer_ids)*target_hidden_size="
                f"{len(capture.aux_hidden_state_layer_ids)}*"
                f"{capture.target_hidden_size}={capture.expected_aux_width}"
            )

    # (4) target last-dim matches target_repr (+ vocab-map dim for pruned_logits)
    expected = capture.expected_target_dim()
    if target_feature_name in shapes and expected is not None:
        shape = tuple(shapes[target_feature_name])
        if not shape:
            raise CaptureMismatchError(
                f"[{sample_id}] target feature {target_feature_name!r} has no dimensions"
            )
        dim = int(shape[-1])
        if dim != expected:
            raise CaptureMismatchError(
                f"[{sample_id}] target last-dim {dim} != expected {expected} "
                f"for target_repr={capture.target_repr!r}"
            )
        if capture.target_repr == "pruned_logits" and capture.vocab_map_version is None:
            raise CaptureMismatchError(
                f"[{sample_id}] target_repr='pruned_logits' requires a "
                f"vocab_map_version so the trainer-side mapping is gated"
            )


def verify_capture(
    tensors: Dict[str, Any],
    capture: CaptureConfig,
    *,
    sample_id: str,
    recorded_aux_layer_ids: Optional[Tuple[int, ...]] = None,
    aux_feature_name: str = "hidden_state",
    target_feature_name: str = "target",
) -> None:
    """Loud, pre-put validation that extracted ``tensors`` match ``capture``.

    Raises :class:`CaptureMismatchError` on the first mismatch with a
    requested-vs-actual diff and the offending ``sample_id``.
    """
    _verify_capture_shapes(
        {name: tuple(tensor.shape) for name, tensor in tensors.items()},
        capture,
        sample_id=sample_id,
        recorded_aux_layer_ids=recorded_aux_layer_ids,
        aux_feature_name=aux_feature_name,
        target_feature_name=target_feature_name,
    )


def verify_capture_specs(
    specs: Mapping[str, FeatureSpec],
    capture: CaptureConfig,
    *,
    sample_id: str,
    recorded_aux_layer_ids: Optional[Tuple[int, ...]] = None,
    aux_feature_name: str = "hidden_state",
    target_feature_name: str = "target",
) -> None:
    """Validate ``FeatureSpec`` metadata against ``capture`` without tensors."""
    _verify_capture_shapes(
        {name: tuple(spec.shape) for name, spec in specs.items()},
        capture,
        sample_id=sample_id,
        recorded_aux_layer_ids=recorded_aux_layer_ids,
        aux_feature_name=aux_feature_name,
        target_feature_name=target_feature_name,
    )


__all__ = [
    "CaptureConfig",
    "CaptureMismatchError",
    "verify_capture",
    "verify_capture_specs",
]

10	
11	An external SGLang server patched with
12	``patches/sglang/v0.5.14/spec-capture.patch`` runs
13	the prefill and writes captured features straight into Mooncake in
14	:class:`MooncakeFeatureStore`'s key layout. Tensors never pass through this
15	process — the ``/generate`` response's ``meta_info["spec_capture"]`` carries
16	only key/shape/dtype, from which :meth:`SGLangServerCaptureAdapter.produce_refs`
17	builds committed-ready ``SampleRef``s.
18	
19	The server knows only generic artifacts (``aux`` = capture layers
20	concatenated, ``last_hidden`` = post-norm final hidden) plus passthrough
21	tensors.  The application composition root injects an algorithm-owned
22	:class:`ServerCaptureSchema`; this transport never resolves an algorithm name.
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import logging
28	import uuid
29	from dataclasses import dataclass
30	from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Mapping, Optional, Tuple, Union
31	
32	from specforge.inference.capture import (
33	    CaptureConfig,
34	    CaptureMismatchError,
35	    verify_capture_specs,
36	)
37	from specforge.runtime.contracts import SCHEMA_VERSION, FeatureSpec, PromptTask
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	@dataclass(frozen=True)
43	class ServerCaptureSchema:
44	    """Maps a strategy's feature names onto the server's capture artifacts.
45	
46	    ``aux_feature`` / ``last_hidden_feature`` name the features fed by the two
47	    engine artifacts (None = not wanted). ``passthrough`` is
48	    ``(feature_name, payload_key, trailing_shape)`` for client tensors stored
49	    verbatim (``trailing_shape`` is appended after ``(1, L)``).
50	    ``attention_mask_feature`` is synthesized all-ones (PromptTasks are unpadded).
51	    """
52	
53	    aux_feature: Optional[str]
54	    last_hidden_feature: Optional[str]
55	    passthrough: Tuple[Tuple[str, str, Tuple[int, ...]], ...]
56	    attention_mask_feature: Optional[str] = None
57	
58	
59	@dataclass(frozen=True)
60	class ServerCaptureFailure:
61	    """Per-task failure from the server transport (worker fails just this task)."""
62	
63	    task_id: str
64	    reason: str
65	    retryable: bool = True
66	
67	
68	def _default_post(url: str, json_body: Dict[str, Any], timeout: float):
69	    import requests
70	
71	    resp = requests.post(url, json=json_body, timeout=timeout)
72	    resp.raise_for_status()
73	    return resp.json()
74	
75	
76	def _flatten_list_wrappers(value: Any) -> List[Any]:
77	    """Flatten list-only response wrappers while leaving row objects intact."""
78	    if not isinstance(value, list):
79	        return [value]
80	    flattened: List[Any] = []
81	    for item in value:
82	        flattened.extend(_flatten_list_wrappers(item))
83	    return flattened
84	
85	
86	def _capture_result_for_task(
87	    value: Any, *, task_id: str, expected_sample_id: str
88	) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
89	    """Select this task's capture from scalar or batch-wrapped results."""
90	    candidates = [item for item in _flatten_list_wrappers(value) if item is not None]
91	    if not candidates:
92	        return None
93	    if not all(isinstance(item, dict) for item in candidates):
94	        types = sorted({type(item).__name__ for item in candidates})
95	        raise RuntimeError(
96	            "spec-capture server returned non-object capture results for task "
97	            f"{task_id}: {types}"
98	        )
99	    if len(candidates) == 1:
100	        return candidates[0]
101	    matches = [
102	        item for item in candidates if str(item.get("sample_id")) == expected_sample_id
103	    ]
104	    if len(matches) != 1:
105	        raise RuntimeError(
106	            "spec-capture server returned an ambiguous batch result for task "
107	            f"{task_id}: expected sample_id={expected_sample_id!r}, "
108	            f"matches={len(matches)}, candidates={len(candidates)}"
109	        )
110	    return matches[0]
111	
112	
113	class SGLangServerCaptureAdapter:
114	    """RefSource over a live spec-capture SGLang server.
115	
116	    Implements ``produce_refs`` (not ``generate_features``): the returned
117	    ``SampleRef``s point at tensors the SERVER already wrote to the Mooncake
118	    store, so the RolloutWorker commits them without any local put. The refs
119	    are verified against the :class:`CaptureConfig` from their FeatureSpecs
120	    alone — same loud extraction-boundary guarantee, no tensor fetch.
121	
122	    ``store`` must be the run's :class:`MooncakeFeatureStore` (its ``store_id``
123	    namespaces the keys and ``adopt()`` registers each ref so a later
124	    ``abort()``/``gc()`` on the producer side can free server-written objects).
125	    ``post_fn`` is injectable for tests.
126	    """
127	
128	    def __init__(
129	        self,
130	        base_url: str,
131	        store,
132	        *,
133	        run_id: str,
134	        algorithm: str,
135	        schema: ServerCaptureSchema,
136	        request_input_adapter=None,
137	        timeout_s: float = 300.0,
138	        post_fn: Optional[Callable[..., Any]] = None,
139	        target_model_version: str = "unknown",
140	    ) -> None:
141	        required_store_api = (
142	            "adopt",
143	            "discard_external_attempts",
144	            "store_id",
145	            "track_external_attempt",
146	        )
147	        missing_store_api = [
148	            name for name in required_store_api if not hasattr(store, name)
149	        ]
150	        if missing_store_api:
151	            raise TypeError(
152	                "SGLangServerCaptureAdapter needs a MooncakeFeatureStore-like "
153	                f"store; missing {missing_store_api}"
154	            )
155	        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
156	        self.store = store
157	        self.run_id = run_id
158	        if not isinstance(schema, ServerCaptureSchema):
159	            raise TypeError("schema must be an injected ServerCaptureSchema")
160	        if not algorithm:
161	            raise ValueError("algorithm must be non-empty")
162	        self.schema = schema
163	        self.strategy = algorithm
164	        if request_input_adapter is not None and not callable(
165	            getattr(request_input_adapter, "build_request_inputs", None)
166	        ):
167	            raise TypeError(
168	                "request_input_adapter must expose build_request_inputs(tasks)"
169	            )
170	        self.request_input_adapter = request_input_adapter
171	        self.timeout_s = timeout_s
172	        self.post_fn = post_fn or _default_post
173	        self.target_model_version = target_model_version
174	        self._healthy = True
175	
176	    # -- request construction -------------------------------------------------
177	    def _sample_id(self, task: PromptTask) -> str:
178	        return f"{self.run_id}:{task.task_id}"
179	
180	    def _request_inputs(self, tasks: List[PromptTask]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
181	        """Build only model-input fields, keeping transport keys runtime-owned."""
182	
183	        if self.request_input_adapter is None:
184	            return {"input_ids": [list(task.payload["input_ids"]) for task in tasks]}
185	        request_inputs = self.request_input_adapter.build_request_inputs(tasks)
186	        if not isinstance(request_inputs, Mapping):
187	            raise TypeError(
188	                "ServerInputAdapter.build_request_inputs must return a mapping"
189	            )
190	        reserved = {"extra_key", "sampling_params", "spec_capture"}
191	        conflicts = sorted(reserved & set(request_inputs))
192	        if conflicts:
193	            raise ValueError(
194	                "ServerInputAdapter cannot set runtime-owned request fields: "
195	                f"{conflicts}"
196	            )
197	        if not request_inputs:
198	            raise ValueError(
199	                "ServerInputAdapter.build_request_inputs returned no model inputs"
200	            )
201	        return dict(request_inputs)
202	
203	    def _spec_capture_payload(self, task: PromptTask) -> Dict[str, Any]:
204	        input_ids = list(task.payload["input_ids"])
205	        length = len(input_ids)
206	        features: Dict[str, str] = {}
207	        if self.schema.aux_feature is not None:
208	            features["aux"] = self.schema.aux_feature
209	        if self.schema.last_hidden_feature is not None:
210	            features["last_hidden"] = self.schema.last_hidden_feature
211	        passthrough: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
212	        for feature_name, payload_key, trailing in self.schema.passthrough:
213	            if payload_key == "input_ids":
214	                data = input_ids
215	            else:
216	                if payload_key not in task.payload:
217	                    raise ValueError(
218	                        f"task {task.task_id}: required capture payload "
219	                        f"{payload_key!r} is missing"
220	                    )
221	                data = list(task.payload[payload_key])
222	            if len(data) != length:
223	                raise ValueError(
224	                    f"task {task.task_id}: payload {payload_key!r} length "
225	                    f"{len(data)} != input_ids length {length}"
226	                )
227	            passthrough.append(
228	                {
229	                    "name": feature_name,
230	                    "data": data,
231	                    "shape": [1, length, *trailing],
232	                    "dtype": "int64",
233	                }
234	            )
235	        if self.schema.attention_mask_feature is not None:
236	            passthrough.append(
237	                {
238	                    "name": self.schema.attention_mask_feature,
239	                    "data": [1] * length,
240	                    "shape": [1, length],
241	                    "dtype": "int64",
242	                }
243	            )
244	        return {
245	            "store_id": self.store.store_id,
246	            "sample_id": self._sample_id(task),
247	            # A task id is unique within a run, and retries happen only before
248	            # its ref is committed.  Keep the generation stable so a response
249	            # lost after the server write cannot strand gN when the retry writes
250	            # gN+1.  The server patch replaces these deterministic keys on a
251	            # retry (``replace``), bounding the attempt to one namespace.
252	            "gen": 1,
253	            "replace": int(task.attempt) > 0,
254	            "features": features,
255	            "passthrough": passthrough,
256	        }
257	
258	    # -- ref construction ------------------------------------------------------
259	    def _ref_from_result(
260	        self, task: PromptTask, result: Dict[str, Any], capture: CaptureConfig
261	    ):
262	        from specforge.runtime.contracts import SampleRef
263	
264	        sample_id = str(result["sample_id"])
265	        gen = int(result["gen"])
266	        feats: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = result["features"]
267	        specs: Dict[str, FeatureSpec] = {}
268	        nbytes = 0
269	        for name, meta in feats.items():
270	            shape = tuple(int(d) for d in meta["shape"])
271	            dtype = str(meta["dtype"])
272	            extra: Dict[str, Any] = {}
273	            if name == self.schema.last_hidden_feature:
274	                extra["target_repr"] = capture.target_repr
275	                if capture.vocab_map_version:
276	                    extra["target_meta"] = {
277	                        "vocab_map_version": capture.vocab_map_version
278	                    }
279	            specs[name] = FeatureSpec(name=name, shape=shape, dtype=dtype, **extra)
280	            nbytes += _spec_nbytes(shape, dtype)
281	        num_tokens = int(task.metadata.get("num_tokens", 0)) or len(
282	            task.payload["input_ids"]
283	        )
284	        return SampleRef(
285	            sample_id=sample_id,
286	            run_id=self.run_id,
287	            source_task_id=task.task_id,
288	            feature_store_uri=f"mooncake://{result['store_id']}/{sample_id}",
289	            feature_keys={n: f"{sample_id}/{n}" for n in specs},
290	            feature_specs=specs,
291	            strategy=self.strategy,
292	            schema_version=SCHEMA_VERSION,
293	            target_model_version=self.target_model_version,
294	            tokenizer_version=str(task.metadata.get("tokenizer_version", "unknown")),
295	            num_tokens=num_tokens,
296	            estimated_bytes=nbytes,
297	            metadata={
298	                "run_id": self.run_id,
299	                "source_task_id": task.task_id,
300	                "strategy": self.strategy,
301	                "target_repr": capture.target_repr,
302	                "vocab_map_version": capture.vocab_map_version,
303	                "transport": "sglang_server_capture",
304	                "server": self.base_url,  # which server captured it (provenance)
305	                "generation": gen,  # the zero-copy get() locator
306	            },
307	        )
308	
309	    # -- the RefSource entry point ----------------------------------------------
310	    def produce_refs(
311	        self, tasks: List[PromptTask], *, capture: CaptureConfig
312	    ) -> List[Union["SampleRef", ServerCaptureFailure]]:  # noqa: F821
313	        """One batched server call -> one committed-ready ref per task.
314	
315	        Order-aligned with ``tasks``; a per-task server error becomes a
316	        :class:`ServerCaptureFailure` (the worker fails just that lease). Any
317	        transport-level error raises — the worker fails the whole lease batch
318	        retryable, mirroring ``generate_features`` semantics.
319	        """
320	        body = self._request_inputs(tasks)
321	        # A fresh cache namespace forces a full prefill on every attempt while
322	        # leaving SGLang free to use the radix data structure required by hybrid
323	        # targets. Retries need a new key because the prior attempt may have
324	        # populated its namespace before the response was lost.
325	        body["extra_key"] = [uuid.uuid4().hex for _ in tasks]
326	        body["sampling_params"] = {"temperature": 0.0, "max_new_tokens": 1}
327	        capture_payloads = [self._spec_capture_payload(t) for t in tasks]
328	        body["spec_capture"] = capture_payloads
329	        for payload in capture_payloads:
330	            feature_names = list(payload["features"].values())
331	            feature_names.extend(
332	                item["name"] for item in payload.get("passthrough", ())
333	            )
334	            self.store.track_external_attempt(
335	                payload["sample_id"],
336	                generation=int(payload["gen"]),
337	                feature_names=feature_names,
338	            )
339	        rows = self.post_fn(
340	            f"{self.base_url}/generate", json_body=body, timeout=self.timeout_s
341	        )
342	        rows = _flatten_list_wrappers(rows)
343	        if len(rows) != len(tasks):
344	            raise RuntimeError(
345	                f"spec-capture server returned {len(rows)} rows for "
346	                f"{len(tasks)} tasks"
347	            )
348	        out: List[Union[Any, ServerCaptureFailure]] = []
349	        successful_refs = []
350	        for task, row in zip(tasks, rows):
351	            if not isinstance(row, dict):
352	                raise RuntimeError(
353	                    "spec-capture server returned a non-object row for task "
354	                    f"{task.task_id}: {type(row).__name__}"
355	                )
356	            meta = row.get("meta_info") or {}
357	            # meta_info["spec_capture"] is the per-request result dict (or an
358	            # {"error": ...} marker) from the server's dedicated output field.
359	            result = _capture_result_for_task(
360	                meta.get("spec_capture"),
361	                task_id=task.task_id,
362	                expected_sample_id=self._sample_id(task),
363	            )
364	            if not result:
365	                out.append(
366	                    ServerCaptureFailure(
367	                        task_id=task.task_id,
368	                        reason=(
369	                            "server_capture: response carries no spec_capture "
370	                            "result — is the server patched and launched with "
371	                            "--enable-spec-capture?"
372	                        ),
373	                        retryable=False,
374	                    )
375	                )
376	                continue
377	            if result.get("error"):
378	                out.append(
379	                    ServerCaptureFailure(
380	                        task_id=task.task_id,
381	                        reason=f"server_capture:{result['error']}",
382	                        retryable=True,
383	                    )
384	                )
385	                continue
386	            expected_identity = {
387	                "sample_id": self._sample_id(task),
388	                "store_id": str(self.store.store_id),
389	                "gen": 1,
390	            }
391	            actual_identity = {
392	                "sample_id": str(result.get("sample_id")),
393	                "store_id": str(result.get("store_id")),
394	                "gen": int(result.get("gen", -1)),
395	            }
396	            if actual_identity != expected_identity:
397	                raise RuntimeError(
398	                    "spec-capture server returned the wrong object identity for "
399	                    f"task {task.task_id}: {actual_identity} != "
400	                    f"{expected_identity}"
401	                )
402	            ref = self._ref_from_result(task, result, capture)
403	            # A capture shorter than the prompt is corrupt: it means cache
404	            # isolation or another full-prefill invariant failed despite the
405	            # request's fresh namespace.
406	            expected_len = len(task.payload["input_ids"])
407	            short = {
408	                name: spec.shape
409	                for name, spec in ref.feature_specs.items()
410	                if len(spec.shape) >= 2 and spec.shape[1] != expected_len
411	            }
412	            if short:
413	                self.store.adopt(ref)
414	                self.store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason="seq-len-mismatch")
415	                out.append(
416	                    ServerCaptureFailure(
417	                        task_id=task.task_id,
418	                        reason=(
419	                            f"server_capture: captured seq len != prompt len "
420	                            f"{expected_len} for {short}; the capture request "
421	                            "did not execute a complete prefill"
422	                        ),
423	                        retryable=False,
424	                    )
425	                )
426	                continue
427	            try:
428	                recorded_aux_layer_ids = result.get("aux_layer_ids")
429	                if (
430	                    capture.aux_hidden_state_layer_ids
431	                    and recorded_aux_layer_ids is None
432	                ):
433	                    raise CaptureMismatchError(
434	                        f"[{ref.sample_id}] capture omitted aux-layer ids; "
435	                        "cannot verify requested layers "
436	                        f"{capture.aux_hidden_state_layer_ids}"
437	                    )
438	                verify_capture_specs(
439	                    ref.feature_specs,
440	                    capture,
441	                    sample_id=ref.sample_id,
442	                    recorded_aux_layer_ids=(
443	                        tuple(recorded_aux_layer_ids)
444	                        if recorded_aux_layer_ids is not None
445	                        else None
446	                    ),
447	                    aux_feature_name=self.schema.aux_feature or "hidden_state",
448	                    target_feature_name=self.schema.last_hidden_feature or "target",
449	                )
450	            except CaptureMismatchError as exc:
451	                # Loud boundary failure; free the server-written keys so a
452	                # mismatched sample is never consumable.
453	                self.store.adopt(ref)
454	                self.store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason=f"contract:{exc}")
455	                out.append(
456	                    ServerCaptureFailure(
457	                        task_id=task.task_id, reason=str(exc), retryable=False
458	                    )
459	                )
460	                continue
461	            # Do not transfer a successful row out of provisional ownership
462	            # until every row in the response has passed structural and
463	            # identity validation.  A later malformed row raises for the whole
464	            # lease batch; adopting this prefix now would make it invisible to
465	            # terminal provisional cleanup even though no refs are returned.
466	            successful_refs.append(ref)
467	            out.append(ref)
468	        for ref in successful_refs:
469	            self.store.adopt(ref)
470	        return out
471	
472	    def health(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
473	        return {
474	            "healthy": self._healthy,
475	            "backend": "sglang_server_capture",
476	            "base_url": self.base_url,
477	            "strategy": self.strategy,
478	        }
479	
480	
481	_DTYPE_BYTES = {
482	    "float64": 8,
483	    "int64": 8,
484	    "float32": 4,
485	    "int32": 4,
486	    "float16": 2,
487	    "bfloat16": 2,
488	    "int16": 2,
489	    "int8": 1,
490	    "uint8": 1,
491	    "bool": 1,
492	}
493	
494	
495	def _spec_nbytes(shape: Tuple[int, ...], dtype: str) -> int:
496	    n = 1
497	    for d in shape:
498	        n *= int(d)
499	    return n * _DTYPE_BYTES.get(dtype, 2)
500	
501	
502	__all__ = [
503	    "ServerCaptureSchema",
504	    "ServerCaptureFailure",
505	    "SGLangServerCaptureAdapter",
506	]
507

# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
#

# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
"""Server-side spec-capture rollout source (zero-copy Mooncake transport).

An external SGLang server patched with
``patches/sglang/v0.5.14/spec-capture.patch`` runs
the prefill and writes captured features straight into Mooncake in
:class:`MooncakeFeatureStore`'s key layout. Tensors never pass through this
process — the ``/generate`` response's ``meta_info["spec_capture"]`` carries
only key/shape/dtype, from which :meth:`SGLangServerCaptureAdapter.produce_refs`
builds committed-ready ``SampleRef``s.

The server knows only generic artifacts (``aux`` = capture layers
concatenated, ``last_hidden`` = post-norm final hidden) plus passthrough
tensors.  The application composition root injects an algorithm-owned
:class:`ServerCaptureSchema`; this transport never resolves an algorithm name.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Mapping, Optional, Tuple, Union

from specforge.inference.capture import (
    CaptureConfig,
    CaptureMismatchError,
    verify_capture_specs,
)
from specforge.runtime.contracts import SCHEMA_VERSION, FeatureSpec, PromptTask

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ServerCaptureSchema:
    """Maps a strategy's feature names onto the server's capture artifacts.

    ``aux_feature`` / ``last_hidden_feature`` name the features fed by the two
    engine artifacts (None = not wanted). ``passthrough`` is
    ``(feature_name, payload_key, trailing_shape)`` for client tensors stored
    verbatim (``trailing_shape`` is appended after ``(1, L)``).
    ``attention_mask_feature`` is synthesized all-ones (PromptTasks are unpadded).
    """

    aux_feature: Optional[str]
    last_hidden_feature: Optional[str]
    passthrough: Tuple[Tuple[str, str, Tuple[int, ...]], ...]
    attention_mask_feature: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ServerCaptureFailure:
    """Per-task failure from the server transport (worker fails just this task)."""

    task_id: str
    reason: str
    retryable: bool = True


def _default_post(url: str, json_body: Dict[str, Any], timeout: float):
    import requests

    resp = requests.post(url, json=json_body, timeout=timeout)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return resp.json()


def _flatten_list_wrappers(value: Any) -> List[Any]:
    """Flatten list-only response wrappers while leaving row objects intact."""
    if not isinstance(value, list):
        return [value]
    flattened: List[Any] = []
    for item in value:
        flattened.extend(_flatten_list_wrappers(item))
    return flattened


def _capture_result_for_task(
    value: Any, *, task_id: str, expected_sample_id: str
) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """Select this task's capture from scalar or batch-wrapped results."""
    candidates = [item for item in _flatten_list_wrappers(value) if item is not None]
    if not candidates:
        return None
    if not all(isinstance(item, dict) for item in candidates):
        types = sorted({type(item).__name__ for item in candidates})
        raise RuntimeError(
            "spec-capture server returned non-object capture results for task "
            f"{task_id}: {types}"
        )
    if len(candidates) == 1:
        return candidates[0]
    matches = [
        item for item in candidates if str(item.get("sample_id")) == expected_sample_id
    ]
    if len(matches) != 1:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "spec-capture server returned an ambiguous batch result for task "
            f"{task_id}: expected sample_id={expected_sample_id!r}, "
            f"matches={len(matches)}, candidates={len(candidates)}"
        )
    return matches[0]


class SGLangServerCaptureAdapter:
    """RefSource over a live spec-capture SGLang server.

    Implements ``produce_refs`` (not ``generate_features``): the returned
    ``SampleRef``s point at tensors the SERVER already wrote to the Mooncake
    store, so the RolloutWorker commits them without any local put. The refs
    are verified against the :class:`CaptureConfig` from their FeatureSpecs
    alone — same loud extraction-boundary guarantee, no tensor fetch.

    ``store`` must be the run's :class:`MooncakeFeatureStore` (its ``store_id``
    namespaces the keys and ``adopt()`` registers each ref so a later
    ``abort()``/``gc()`` on the producer side can free server-written objects).
    ``post_fn`` is injectable for tests.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        base_url: str,
        store,
        *,
        run_id: str,
        algorithm: str,
        schema: ServerCaptureSchema,
        request_input_adapter=None,
        timeout_s: float = 300.0,
        post_fn: Optional[Callable[..., Any]] = None,
        target_model_version: str = "unknown",
    ) -> None:
        required_store_api = (
            "adopt",
            "discard_external_attempts",
            "store_id",
            "track_external_attempt",
        )
        missing_store_api = [
            name for name in required_store_api if not hasattr(store, name)
        ]
        if missing_store_api:
            raise TypeError(
                "SGLangServerCaptureAdapter needs a MooncakeFeatureStore-like "
                f"store; missing {missing_store_api}"
            )
        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
        self.store = store
        self.run_id = run_id
        if not isinstance(schema, ServerCaptureSchema):
            raise TypeError("schema must be an injected ServerCaptureSchema")
        if not algorithm:
            raise ValueError("algorithm must be non-empty")
        self.schema = schema
        self.strategy = algorithm
        if request_input_adapter is not None and not callable(
            getattr(request_input_adapter, "build_request_inputs", None)
        ):
            raise TypeError(
                "request_input_adapter must expose build_request_inputs(tasks)"
            )
        self.request_input_adapter = request_input_adapter
        self.timeout_s = timeout_s
        self.post_fn = post_fn or _default_post
        self.target_model_version = target_model_version
        self._healthy = True

    # -- request construction -------------------------------------------------
    def _sample_id(self, task: PromptTask) -> str:
        return f"{self.run_id}:{task.task_id}"

    def _request_inputs(self, tasks: List[PromptTask]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Build only model-input fields, keeping transport keys runtime-owned."""

        if self.request_input_adapter is None:
            return {"input_ids": [list(task.payload["input_ids"]) for task in tasks]}
        request_inputs = self.request_input_adapter.build_request_inputs(tasks)
        if not isinstance(request_inputs, Mapping):
            raise TypeError(
                "ServerInputAdapter.build_request_inputs must return a mapping"
            )
        reserved = {"extra_key", "sampling_params", "spec_capture"}
        conflicts = sorted(reserved & set(request_inputs))
        if conflicts:
            raise ValueError(
                "ServerInputAdapter cannot set runtime-owned request fields: "
                f"{conflicts}"
            )
        if not request_inputs:
            raise ValueError(
                "ServerInputAdapter.build_request_inputs returned no model inputs"
            )
        return dict(request_inputs)

    def _spec_capture_payload(self, task: PromptTask) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        input_ids = list(task.payload["input_ids"])
        length = len(input_ids)
        features: Dict[str, str] = {}
        if self.schema.aux_feature is not None:
            features["aux"] = self.schema.aux_feature
        if self.schema.last_hidden_feature is not None:
            features["last_hidden"] = self.schema.last_hidden_feature
        passthrough: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
        for feature_name, payload_key, trailing in self.schema.passthrough:
            if payload_key == "input_ids":
                data = input_ids
            else:
                if payload_key not in task.payload:
                    raise ValueError(
                        f"task {task.task_id}: required capture payload "
                        f"{payload_key!r} is missing"
                    )
                data = list(task.payload[payload_key])
            if len(data) != length:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"task {task.task_id}: payload {payload_key!r} length "
                    f"{len(data)} != input_ids length {length}"
                )
            passthrough.append(
                {
                    "name": feature_name,
                    "data": data,
                    "shape": [1, length, *trailing],
                    "dtype": "int64",
                }
            )
        if self.schema.attention_mask_feature is not None:
            passthrough.append(
                {
                    "name": self.schema.attention_mask_feature,
                    "data": [1] * length,
                    "shape": [1, length],
                    "dtype": "int64",
                }
            )
        return {
            "store_id": self.store.store_id,
            "sample_id": self._sample_id(task),
            # A task id is unique within a run, and retries happen only before
            # its ref is committed.  Keep the generation stable so a response
            # lost after the server write cannot strand gN when the retry writes
            # gN+1.  The server patch replaces these deterministic keys on a
            # retry (``replace``), bounding the attempt to one namespace.
            "gen": 1,
            "replace": int(task.attempt) > 0,
            "features": features,
            "passthrough": passthrough,
        }

    # -- ref construction ------------------------------------------------------
    def _ref_from_result(
        self, task: PromptTask, result: Dict[str, Any], capture: CaptureConfig
    ):
        from specforge.runtime.contracts import SampleRef

        sample_id = str(result["sample_id"])
        gen = int(result["gen"])
        feats: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = result["features"]
        specs: Dict[str, FeatureSpec] = {}
        nbytes = 0
        for name, meta in feats.items():
            shape = tuple(int(d) for d in meta["shape"])
            dtype = str(meta["dtype"])
            extra: Dict[str, Any] = {}
            if name == self.schema.last_hidden_feature:
                extra["target_repr"] = capture.target_repr
                if capture.vocab_map_version:
                    extra["target_meta"] = {
                        "vocab_map_version": capture.vocab_map_version
                    }
            specs[name] = FeatureSpec(name=name, shape=shape, dtype=dtype, **extra)
            nbytes += _spec_nbytes(shape, dtype)
        num_tokens = int(task.metadata.get("num_tokens", 0)) or len(
            task.payload["input_ids"]
        )
        return SampleRef(
            sample_id=sample_id,
            run_id=self.run_id,
            source_task_id=task.task_id,
            feature_store_uri=f"mooncake://{result['store_id']}/{sample_id}",
            feature_keys={n: f"{sample_id}/{n}" for n in specs},
            feature_specs=specs,
            strategy=self.strategy,
            schema_version=SCHEMA_VERSION,
            target_model_version=self.target_model_version,
            tokenizer_version=str(task.metadata.get("tokenizer_version", "unknown")),
            num_tokens=num_tokens,
            estimated_bytes=nbytes,
            metadata={
                "run_id": self.run_id,
                "source_task_id": task.task_id,
                "strategy": self.strategy,
                "target_repr": capture.target_repr,
                "vocab_map_version": capture.vocab_map_version,
                "transport": "sglang_server_capture",
                "server": self.base_url,  # which server captured it (provenance)
                "generation": gen,  # the zero-copy get() locator
            },
        )

    # -- the RefSource entry point ----------------------------------------------
    def produce_refs(
        self, tasks: List[PromptTask], *, capture: CaptureConfig
    ) -> List[Union["SampleRef", ServerCaptureFailure]]:  # noqa: F821
        """One batched server call -> one committed-ready ref per task.

        Order-aligned with ``tasks``; a per-task server error becomes a
        :class:`ServerCaptureFailure` (the worker fails just that lease). Any
        transport-level error raises — the worker fails the whole lease batch
        retryable, mirroring ``generate_features`` semantics.
        """
        body = self._request_inputs(tasks)
        # A fresh cache namespace forces a full prefill on every attempt while
        # leaving SGLang free to use the radix data structure required by hybrid
        # targets. Retries need a new key because the prior attempt may have
        # populated its namespace before the response was lost.
        body["extra_key"] = [uuid.uuid4().hex for _ in tasks]
        body["sampling_params"] = {"temperature": 0.0, "max_new_tokens": 1}
        capture_payloads = [self._spec_capture_payload(t) for t in tasks]
        body["spec_capture"] = capture_payloads
        for payload in capture_payloads:
            feature_names = list(payload["features"].values())
            feature_names.extend(
                item["name"] for item in payload.get("passthrough", ())
            )
            self.store.track_external_attempt(
                payload["sample_id"],
                generation=int(payload["gen"]),
                feature_names=feature_names,
            )
        rows = self.post_fn(
            f"{self.base_url}/generate", json_body=body, timeout=self.timeout_s
        )
        rows = _flatten_list_wrappers(rows)
        if len(rows) != len(tasks):
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"spec-capture server returned {len(rows)} rows for "
                f"{len(tasks)} tasks"
            )
        out: List[Union[Any, ServerCaptureFailure]] = []
        successful_refs = []
        for task, row in zip(tasks, rows):
            if not isinstance(row, dict):
                raise RuntimeError(
                    "spec-capture server returned a non-object row for task "
                    f"{task.task_id}: {type(row).__name__}"
                )
            meta = row.get("meta_info") or {}
            # meta_info["spec_capture"] is the per-request result dict (or an
            # {"error": ...} marker) from the server's dedicated output field.
            result = _capture_result_for_task(
                meta.get("spec_capture"),
                task_id=task.task_id,
                expected_sample_id=self._sample_id(task),
            )
            if not result:
                out.append(
                    ServerCaptureFailure(
                        task_id=task.task_id,
                        reason=(
                            "server_capture: response carries no spec_capture "
                            "result — is the server patched and launched with "
                            "--enable-spec-capture?"
                        ),
                        retryable=False,
                    )
                )
                continue
            if result.get("error"):
                out.append(
                    ServerCaptureFailure(
                        task_id=task.task_id,
                        reason=f"server_capture:{result['error']}",
                        retryable=True,
                    )
                )
                continue
            expected_identity = {
                "sample_id": self._sample_id(task),
                "store_id": str(self.store.store_id),
                "gen": 1,
            }
            actual_identity = {
                "sample_id": str(result.get("sample_id")),
                "store_id": str(result.get("store_id")),
                "gen": int(result.get("gen", -1)),
            }
            if actual_identity != expected_identity:
                raise RuntimeError(
                    "spec-capture server returned the wrong object identity for "
                    f"task {task.task_id}: {actual_identity} != "
                    f"{expected_identity}"
                )
            ref = self._ref_from_result(task, result, capture)
            # A capture shorter than the prompt is corrupt: it means cache
            # isolation or another full-prefill invariant failed despite the
            # request's fresh namespace.
            expected_len = len(task.payload["input_ids"])
            short = {
                name: spec.shape
                for name, spec in ref.feature_specs.items()
                if len(spec.shape) >= 2 and spec.shape[1] != expected_len
            }
            if short:
                self.store.adopt(ref)
                self.store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason="seq-len-mismatch")
                out.append(
                    ServerCaptureFailure(
                        task_id=task.task_id,
                        reason=(
                            f"server_capture: captured seq len != prompt len "
                            f"{expected_len} for {short}; the capture request "
                            "did not execute a complete prefill"
                        ),
                        retryable=False,
                    )
                )
                continue
            try:
                recorded_aux_layer_ids = result.get("aux_layer_ids")
                if (
                    capture.aux_hidden_state_layer_ids
                    and recorded_aux_layer_ids is None
                ):
                    raise CaptureMismatchError(
                        f"[{ref.sample_id}] capture omitted aux-layer ids; "
                        "cannot verify requested layers "
                        f"{capture.aux_hidden_state_layer_ids}"
                    )
                verify_capture_specs(
                    ref.feature_specs,
                    capture,
                    sample_id=ref.sample_id,
                    recorded_aux_layer_ids=(
                        tuple(recorded_aux_layer_ids)
                        if recorded_aux_layer_ids is not None
                        else None
                    ),
                    aux_feature_name=self.schema.aux_feature or "hidden_state",
                    target_feature_name=self.schema.last_hidden_feature or "target",
                )
            except CaptureMismatchError as exc:
                # Loud boundary failure; free the server-written keys so a
                # mismatched sample is never consumable.
                self.store.adopt(ref)
                self.store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason=f"contract:{exc}")
                out.append(
                    ServerCaptureFailure(
                        task_id=task.task_id, reason=str(exc), retryable=False
                    )
                )
                continue
            # Do not transfer a successful row out of provisional ownership
            # until every row in the response has passed structural and
            # identity validation.  A later malformed row raises for the whole
            # lease batch; adopting this prefix now would make it invisible to
            # terminal provisional cleanup even though no refs are returned.
            successful_refs.append(ref)
            out.append(ref)
        for ref in successful_refs:
            self.store.adopt(ref)
        return out

    def health(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "healthy": self._healthy,
            "backend": "sglang_server_capture",
            "base_url": self.base_url,
            "strategy": self.strategy,
        }


_DTYPE_BYTES = {
    "float64": 8,
    "int64": 8,
    "float32": 4,
    "int32": 4,
    "float16": 2,
    "bfloat16": 2,
    "int16": 2,
    "int8": 1,
    "uint8": 1,
    "bool": 1,
}


def _spec_nbytes(shape: Tuple[int, ...], dtype: str) -> int:
    n = 1
    for d in shape:
        n *= int(d)
    return n * _DTYPE_BYTES.get(dtype, 2)


__all__ = [
    "ServerCaptureSchema",
    "ServerCaptureFailure",
    "SGLangServerCaptureAdapter",
]

file.content:
# coding=utf-8
"""Inference / rollout plane: rollout worker, capture config, adapters, target engines.

Submodules import the SpecForge model / SGLang code, so they are imported
explicitly by callers rather than at package load.
"""

content.content:
1	# coding=utf-8
2	# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	"""Target-batch ownership for colocated tensor-parallel rollout."""
10	
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	from dataclasses import dataclass
14	from typing import Sequence, TypeVar
15	
16	_T = TypeVar("_T")
17	
18	
19	@dataclass(frozen=True)
20	class TargetBatchPartition:
21	    """One rank's contiguous share of a target-TP capture batch.
22	
23	    Every rank in a target tensor-parallel group captures the same logical
24	    batch.  The frozen target therefore sees ``size * local_batch_size``
25	    prompts, while this rank commits and trains only its contiguous share.
26	    """
27	
28	    rank: int = 0
29	    size: int = 1
30	
31	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
32	        if self.size < 1:
33	            raise ValueError(f"target TP size must be >= 1, got {self.size}")
34	        if not 0 <= self.rank < self.size:
35	            raise ValueError(f"target TP rank {self.rank} is outside [0, {self.size})")
36	
37	    @classmethod
38	    def from_distributed(cls, configured_size: int) -> "TargetBatchPartition":
39	        """Resolve the local target-TP rank and verify the configured group."""
40	        import torch.distributed as dist
41	
42	        configured_size = int(configured_size)
43	        if configured_size < 1:
44	            raise ValueError(
45	                f"configured target TP size must be >= 1, got {configured_size}"
46	            )
47	        if not (dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized()):
48	            if configured_size != 1:
49	                raise RuntimeError(
50	                    "target tp_size > 1 requires an initialized distributed "
51	                    "process group"
52	                )
53	            return cls()
54	
55	        from specforge.distributed import get_tp_group
56	
57	        group = get_tp_group()
58	        actual_size = dist.get_world_size(group)
59	        if actual_size != configured_size:
60	            raise ValueError(
61	                "configured target TP size does not match the initialized group: "
62	                f"configured={configured_size}, actual={actual_size}"
63	            )
64	        return cls(rank=dist.get_rank(group), size=actual_size)
65	
66	    def target_batch_size(self, local_batch_size: int) -> int:
67	        if local_batch_size < 0:
68	            raise ValueError(
69	                f"local batch size must be non-negative, got {local_batch_size}"
70	            )
71	        return self.size * local_batch_size
72	
73	    def local_slice(self, target_batch_size: int) -> slice:
74	        if target_batch_size % self.size:
75	            raise ValueError(
76	                f"target batch size {target_batch_size} is not divisible by "
77	                f"target TP size {self.size}"
78	            )
79	        local_batch_size = target_batch_size // self.size
80	        start = self.rank * local_batch_size
81	        return slice(start, start + local_batch_size)
82	
83	    def select(self, values: Sequence[_T]) -> list[_T]:
84	        return list(values[self.local_slice(len(values))])
85	
86	
87	__all__ = ["TargetBatchPartition"]
88
